successCriteria.description: src/modules/audit/audit.model.ts exists and imports AuditRecord, CreateAuditRecordDto from ../../shared/types/index, re-exports them, and exports AuditAction enum
src/modules/audit/audit.repository.ts exists and exports IAuditRepository (with findByEntity and create methods) and PgAuditRepository (extends BaseRepository<AuditRecord>)
src/modules/audit/audit.service.ts exists and exports IAuditService (with recordAction method) and AuditService (depends on IAuditRepository)
IAuditRepository.findByEntity(entityType, entityId) returns all audit records for that entity ordered by performed_at DESC
AuditService.recordAction constructs a CreateAuditRecordDto from its parameters and calls auditRepository.create, returning the created AuditRecord
PgAuditRepository.create inserts a row into audit_records using the pool and returns the created AuditRecord with generated id, performed_at, and created_at
All audit record creations are atomic and use the database connection from src/shared/db/connection.ts
Jest unit tests exist in tests/unit/modules/audit/audit.repository.test.ts covering PgAuditRepository.create and findByEntity
Jest unit tests exist in tests/unit/modules/audit/audit.service.test.ts covering AuditService.recordAction with a mocked IAuditRepository

constraints: Repository pattern: All database access goes through repository interfaces.
Audit records: All state-changing operations write an audit record (this module implements the audit record mechanism).
No sensitive data in logs: Never log passwords, tokens, PII, or financial data.
Error handling: No unhandled promise rejections. All async errors are caught and handled.
No API surface — purely programmatic, called by other services.
Audit module must not depend on any other domain modules (employee, policy, balance, leave, notification).
outOfScope: Any other modules not explicitly mentioned
Integration tests, e2e tests, configuration files beyond the module files
